steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is Command of Evidence (finding a quotation that best illustrates a claim). The claim is that Newland "visualizes the details of a future with May" — the correct quotation must show active mental imagination of an upcoming life with May, not present-moment observation at the opera.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "[May] dropped her eyes to the immense bouquet...": describes Newland observing May's physical actions in the present moment at the opera. No future visualization is shown. ✗
  • Choice B — "Newland Archer, leaning against the wall...turned his eyes from the stage and scanned the opposite side of the house.": describes Newland looking around the opera house in the present. He is observing, not imagining a future. ✗
  • Choice C — "Though there was already talk...of a new Opera House...": describes the opera house setting and society's attitude toward it. Newland is not mentioned and no future with May is visualized. ✗
  • Choice D — "Already [Newland's] imagination, leaping ahead of the engagement ring, the betrothal kiss and the [wedding] march from Lohengrin, pictured [May] at his side.": the words "imagination" and "pictured" directly show Newland mentally projecting forward to specific future milestones with May. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
